title: dortmanna appendiculata
intro: Dortmanna appendiculata, also known as the dodder, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dortmanna appendiculata include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as acne and eczema. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
